title: Cisco Supplier Guide + Global Human Rights Policy — Forced Labor & Due Diligence

metadata.source_document: Cisco Supplier Guide + Global Human Rights Policy EDCS-1210115
metadata.review_guidance: Suppliers must prohibit forced labor in any form, cascade Code standards on prohibition of forced labor and definition of fees, conduct due diligence on recruitment agencies, and maintain OECD-aligned due diligence program.
metadata.preferred_position: All suppliers must have a due diligence program aligned with OECD due diligence guidelines. Cisco prohibits forced labor in any form including bonded labor, involuntary prison labor, slavery or trafficking of persons.

sections.title: Prohibition of Forced Labor
sections.text: Maintain compliance and help Cisco comply with applicable laws regarding forced labor due diligence and modern slavery. Cascade the Code standards on Prohibition of Forced Labor and Definition of Fees to suppliers, recruitment agencies, and labor service providers. Conduct due diligence on recruitment agencies and labor service providers to ensure compliance. All suppliers must have a due diligence program aligned with the OECD due diligence guidelines.

sections.title: Global Human Rights Policy — Forced Labor Commitment
sections.text: Cisco prohibits forced labor in any form, including but not limited to bonded or indentured labor, involuntary or exploitative prison labor, slavery or trafficking of persons. Cisco expects partners and suppliers to adopt commitments aligned to the UN Guiding Principles, UDHR, and eight core ILO Conventions including the Forced Labour Convention and Abolition of Forced Labour Convention.
